texto: They say your hardcore's not yours, and it's not fate. Nobody's gonna tell you what shit like that means.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Noah Wareness: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “They say your hardcore's not yours, and it's not fate. Nobody's gonna tell you what shit like tha…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, destiny, determinism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de determinism e determinismo.

frase_original: They say your hardcore's not yours, and it's not fate. Nobody's gonna tell you what shit like that means.

texto: You know how long's the universe. It's seven million freeways side by side. You know how high. So high the moon just falls. But little punks, you still know the hardcore of the universe. It's cause you're hardcore too. You're made of pure universe, under your bones. And nothing ever starts shit by meaning to. We meant to break down the amerikan dream throwing bottles. All we broke was bottles. What worked was one stolen handful of flax seed. All ages shows in the highschool parkade, and the keys to the bandroom door. Their dream was a joke anyway. What worked was a nother joke.
explicacao: Citação de Noah Wareness sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “You know how long's the universe. It's seven million freeways side by side. You know how high. So…” articula reflexao, anarchism, life-during-wartime em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e life-during-wartime.

frase_original: You know how long's the universe. It's seven million freeways side by side. You know how high. So high the moon just falls. But little punks, you still know the hardcore of the universe. It's cause you're hardcore too. You're made of pure universe, under your bones. And nothing ever starts shit by meaning to. We meant to break down the amerikan dream throwing bottles. All we broke was bottles. What worked was one stolen handful of flax seed. All ages shows in the highschool parkade, and the keys to the bandroom door. Their dream was a joke anyway. What worked was a nother joke.

texto: The patches are the stories. Hold onto that. And the muddy zigzag of ducktape against the cracked doorglass. There's four kids who sleep here, a nuff for the fingers on each otherses hands. There's room in each of them for one important thing. They're a band. It's not they're in a band. They're a band. Four spikes of ducktape, up and down, like mountain peaks or a sawblade. Every band's got a sign, something to sew on your jacket, gouge on the wall at a show. Four spikes up and down say MEATHEADS, and you picked a fucked window to knock at, tourist. They're the best band in the world.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Noah Wareness: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “The patches are the stories. Hold onto that. And the muddy zigzag of ducktape against the cracked…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, bands, broken-glass pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e broken-glass.

frase_original: The patches are the stories. Hold onto that. And the muddy zigzag of ducktape against the cracked doorglass. There's four kids who sleep here, a nuff for the fingers on each otherses hands. There's room in each of them for one important thing. They're a band. It's not they're in a band. They're a band. Four spikes of ducktape, up and down, like mountain peaks or a sawblade. Every band's got a sign, something to sew on your jacket, gouge on the wall at a show. Four spikes up and down say MEATHEADS, and you picked a fucked window to knock at, tourist. They're the best band in the world.
